name: AT&T Inc.
lede: AT&T runs four capability centers across four Indian cities. Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai and Gurugram, covering gbs and R&D, built over 26 years.
insight: Search elsewhere and you will usually find one AT&T office in India. We map four, across four cities, each tagged by center type and function. Knowing the whole footprint is what turns a name on a list into an account you can plan against.
metaTitle: AT&T Inc. GCC in India: Centers, Footprint & Leaders | Bamboo Reports
metaDescription: AT&T runs 4 verified GCC centers across 4 Indian cities (Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai, Gurugram), covering gbs and R&D. See the full India footprint, org map and contacts on Bamboo Reports.

leaders.departments: Finance, IT, HR, GCC leadership and Procurement

titles.title: CEO AT&T Communication Services India, VP India Development Center

about.description: AT&T's India footprint covers Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai and Gurugram, spanning gbs and R&D. The centers deliver IT, Finance & Accounting, HR and Procurement for the group's global operations.

bandCopy: Exact center locations, per-center headcount, the technology stack and all 109 decision-makers, in one profile.

faq.q: How many GCC centers does AT&T operate in India?
faq.a: AT&T operates 4 active Global Capability Center sites in India.

faq.q: Which Indian cities does AT&T have GCC centers in?
faq.a: AT&T's India GCC footprint spans Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai and Gurugram.

faq.q: What types of GCC centers does AT&T run in India?
faq.a: AT&T runs gbs and R&D centers in India.

faq.q: How many people work at AT&T's India GCCs?
faq.a: AT&T's India centers employ in the 5,000 to 10,000 range. The exact aggregate and the headcount at each site are in the full profile.

faq.q: Who leads AT&T's GCC in India?
faq.a: Bamboo Reports tracks 109 leaders at AT&T's India centers, across Finance, IT, HR, GCC leadership and Procurement. Names and contact details open with a free account.

sources: company filings, LinkedIn, NASSCOM and Bamboo Reports' primary research
